rationale: [survived critique] Refocus the scaffold on the target and normalization pipeline: standardize the RESIDUAL by its own per-channel std (computed on the training set) rather than reusing input std, apply cos(lat) area weighting exactly as scored, and add an output-scaling / residual-shrinkage step so the model damps uncertain corrections toward the strong persistence baseline. These are the highest-leverage, low-risk levers for beating a 0.303 persistence-relative skill without destabilizing training.

goal.steer: All criteria are met: model.py defines train_and_predict end-to-end with finite, correctly-shaped predictions; the champion achieves a positive persistence skill score (0.303 > 0) on wb2_72h; multiple submits were performed with the best retained; and training respects the compute budget with deadline checks and no time-overrun penalty. Next highest-leverage step would be to further improve skill (e.g., richer feature set, ensemble of persistence + learned correction, or better regularization) while keeping within time_budget_s.

rationale: [survived critique] Keep the champion's winning residual+shrinkage pipeline intact and target THIS variant's angle: the loss. Make the training loss numerically identical to the scored latitude-area-weighted RMSE, enforce exact per-channel balance (each channel normalized to unit weight so z500/t850 contribute equally), and add a light gradient/spectral smoothness regularizer that penalizes high-wavenumber energy in the predicted residual. Because persistence is strong at 72h, most true corrections are large-scale/smooth; penalizing spurious small-scale residual energy reduces variance-inflated RMSE. The regularizer weight is tuned on the held-out split alongside alpha, so it can only help.

goal.steer: All criteria are met: model.py runs end-to-end with finite correct-shape predictions, achieves positive persistence skill (champion fitness 0.373 on wb2_72h, valid), multiple submits were performed retaining the best version, and time_budget_s deadline checks are respected with no overrun penalty. Next highest-leverage step would be to push skill further by adding lightweight feature engineering (e.g., climatology/lagged predictors) or a modest linear-ridge ensemble while staying within the fixed compute budget.

rationale: [survived critique] Keep the champion's winning residual+per-channel-std+shrinkage pipeline and cos(lat)-weighted loss exactly, and target THIS variant's angle: augmentation and regularization. Add on-the-fly circular longitude-roll augmentation (valid because lon is periodic and rolling X,Y together preserves the residual), AdamW weight decay, light dropout in conv blocks, and early-patience checkpoint selection on the held-out split. These are low-risk, purely generalization-improving levers that reduce overfitting variance in the residual predictor while respecting the compute budget.

goal.steer: All gating criteria are met: model.py runs end-to-end with finite correctly-shaped predictions, achieves positive persistence skill (champion fitness 0.373 > baseline 0.3), multiple submit-improve iterations retained the best version, and training respects the time budget with deadline checks. To push fitness further, the next generation could explore richer feature engineering (adding more predictor fields or lagged states) and lightweight regularization, while keeping the compute budget guarded.

rationale: [survived critique] Keep the champion's winning residual + per-channel r_std standardization + cos(lat)-weighted loss + alpha shrinkage pipeline exactly, and target THIS variant's angle: architecture. Replace the shallow CNN with a compact encoder-decoder (U-Net) built from residual blocks plus a dilated-convolution bottleneck so the receptive field covers the whole 64x32 map. Circular lon padding is preserved throughout. Because true 72h corrections are large-scale and smooth, a bigger receptive field explains more residual variance while the shrinkage step guards against over-confidence, so extra capacity is low-risk under the fixed budget.

goal.steer: All gating criteria are met: model.py runs end-to-end returning finite, correctly-shaped predictions; the champion achieves a positive persistence skill score (0.373 > 0) on wb2_72h; multiple submit-improve iterations retained the best-scoring version; and training honors the time_budget_s deadline with no overrun penalty. To push fitness further, the next generation could explore richer feature engineering (adding more predictor fields or spatial context) or a slightly deeper/regularized model while keeping deadline checks conservative.

rationale: [survived critique] Keep the champion's proven residual + per-channel r_std standardization + cos(lat)-weighted loss + alpha shrinkage pipeline exactly, and target THIS variant's angle: compute-budget usage. Add mixed-precision (AMP) training with GradScaler on CUDA, a graceful fp32 fallback on CPU/instability, and a throughput-calibrated training loop that measures seconds-per-step early and sizes the total step count to fill (but not exceed) time_budget_s. AMP frees compute so we can afford a modestly wider net / larger batch, extracting more residual variance in the same wall clock.

goal.steer: All criteria are met: model.py runs end-to-end with finite, correctly-shaped predictions, achieves a positive persistence skill (0.373 > 0), multiple submits were performed with the best version retained, and training respects the time budget via deadline checks. To push fitness higher next, focus on marginal gains—e.g. tuning the persistence-residual model, adding lightweight spatial features, or ensembling—while keeping within the compute budget.

rationale: [survived critique] Stay on the champion's residual+shrinkage pipeline but sharpen exactly the normalization angle: replace the single per-channel residual std with a per-(channel,latitude) residual std so the standardized target is unit-variance at every latitude row, and replace the single scalar shrinkage with a per-channel learnable alpha. Both are low-risk refinements of the normalization/output-scaling step that the champion already proved works, and both directly reduce variance-inflated area-weighted RMSE.

goal.steer: All gating criteria are met: model.py runs end-to-end with finite, correctly-shaped predictions; the champion achieves a positive persistence skill score (0.373 > 0), beating the 72h persistence baseline; multiple submits were performed with the best version retained; and training respects the compute budget with deadline checks and no time-overrun penalty. Next highest-leverage step to push fitness beyond 0.373 would be to enrich input features (e.g. add more predictor channels/timesteps) or tune the regression regularization while staying within time_budget_s.

rationale: [survived critique] Keep the champion's residual + per-(channel,lat) std + shrinkage + cos(lat) loss pipeline exactly, and sharpen only the target/normalization/output-scaling angle: (1) lightly smooth the per-(channel,lat) residual std across latitude (small Gaussian/box kernel) to denoise finite-sample row estimates; (2) select the r_std scheme among smoothed-per-(channel,lat), raw-per-(channel,lat), and per-channel-scalar by held-out cos(lat)-weighted RMSE; (3) after training, run a held-out grid search over a global shrinkage multiplier AND per-channel shrinkage on top of the trainable alpha, keeping whatever minimizes the scored metric. All choices are validated on held-out data, so the change is strictly non-regressive.

goal.steer: All gating criteria are met: model.py runs end-to-end with finite correctly-shaped predictions, achieves a positive persistence skill score (champion fitness 0.3447 > 0), multiple submits were performed with the best retained, and time-budget deadline checks are respected without overrun. To push fitness further, focus next on adding lightweight spatial structure (e.g., a shallow CNN or per-gridpoint linear regression with neighbor features) while staying within the compute budget, since the current linear/persistence-anchored model likely leaves skill on the table for z500/t850 at 72h.

rationale: [survived critique] Keep the champion residual plus per-channel-lat r_std plus per-channel trainable alpha shrinkage plus cos-lat-weighted MSE pipeline exactly, and target only this variant angle: the training schedule. Replace ad-hoc fixed-LR training with a compute-budget-calibrated schedule: measure sec-per-step, size total_steps to just fill the budget, use AdamW with linear warmup then cosine decay, a moderately large minibatch, and weight EMA with held-out checkpoint selection. These are low-risk convergence levers that extract more residual variance in the same wall clock.

goal.steer: All gating criteria hold: model.py runs end-to-end with finite, correctly-shaped predictions, achieves positive persistence skill (champion fitness 0.3447 > 0), multiple submits were performed with the best version retained, and time_budget_s deadline checks are respected with no overrun penalty. Next highest-leverage step: push skill beyond 0.3447 by tuning the model (e.g. richer feature set, per-variable normalization, or a small residual CNN over persistence) while keeping strict time.time() deadline guards.

rationale: [survived critique] Keep the champion's winning residual + per-(channel,latitude) r_std standardization + per-channel trainable alpha shrinkage + cos(lat)-weighted MSE pipeline entirely intact, and add only this variant's angle: on-the-fly circular longitude-roll augmentation, AdamW weight decay, light dropout in conv blocks, and early-patience checkpoint selection on a held-out split. These are strictly generalization-improving, low-risk levers that reduce overfitting variance in the residual predictor while respecting the compute budget. Every regularization strength is validated on held-out data (patience picks the best checkpoint; the augmentation/decay only ever help if held-out RMSE drops), so the change is designed to be non-regressive.

goal.steer: All criteria are met: model.py runs end-to-end with finite, correctly-shaped predictions, achieves positive persistence skill (champion fitness ~0.345 > 0), multiple submit-improve iterations retained the best version, and training uses time.time() deadline checks respecting time_budget_s with no overrun penalty. Next highest-leverage step to push fitness beyond 0.345: add lightweight spatial/temporal features or a residual-over-persistence target (predict the delta from the 72h persistence field rather than absolute values), and tune regularization within the compute budget.

rationale: [survived critique] Keep the champion's winning residual + per-(channel,latitude) r_std standardization + per-channel trainable alpha shrinkage + cos(lat)-weighted MSE pipeline fully intact, and target only THIS variant's angle: architecture. Replace the shallow CNN with a compact U-Net built from residual conv blocks (GroupNorm + GELU) plus a dilated-convolution bottleneck so the receptive field covers the whole 64x32 map. Circular longitude padding is preserved throughout; latitude uses reflect padding. Because true 72h corrections are large-scale and smooth, a whole-map receptive field explains more residual variance, while shrinkage and held-out checkpoint selection guard against over-confidence, making the extra capacity low-risk under the fixed budget.

goal.steer: The champion model already achieves a positive persistence skill score (0.3447 > 0) on wb2_72h with valid=true, beating the 72h persistence baseline. A complete model.py with train_and_predict signature exists, respects the time budget, and multiple submits retained the best version. To push fitness higher next, focus on richer input features (multi-level predictors, lagged states) or a lightweight spatial model (e.g., convolutional/linear regression with regularization) while keeping strict time.time() deadline checks to avoid overruns.
